threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 149 bus takes you anywhere useful after 10 PM. Welcome to the club of late-night chaos and questionable kebab choices.
Last night on the 196, a guy in a bright orange tracksuit tried to sell his mixtape to a sleeping passenger. The mixtape was still in his hands when he got off at Tulse Hill.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a family of wild boars casually crossing the street on Sonnenallee. Just another day in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those impromptu street concerts? And how the BVG buses just roll on by like it’s business as usual? Classic Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name at least three different taco trucks and passionately debate why La Taqueria has the best burrito.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus in the Tenderloin where a guy serenaded everyone with an out-of-tune ukulele. No one clapped, but the vibe was strangely uplifting.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountain at Plaça de la Vila is just for selfies, but locals use it as a meeting point for spontaneous jam sessions, especially on weekends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a newbie by their fresh, unwrinkled metro map—still clutching it like a lifeline. Locals just swipe their cards and go.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver shouts 'move down inside!' like it’s a foreign language.
Caught the N88 last night; a guy was serenading the driver with a guitar. Everyone just sat there, half amused, half confused. Only in Clapham!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids making a rap video on the corner by REWE while the old couple next door argued about their garden. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can take a midnight U-Bahn ride and share the train with a DJ spinning tunes on a portable deck. Only in Berlin do parties roll through the underground!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you’re the only one unfazed by a BART delay and still make it to Tacolicious before 8.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy in a tuxedo jump on the 47, still holding a champagne flute. Only in SF do fancy soirées collide with the daily grind.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just pop into a bar and get a seat anywhere. Locals know to arrive early or risk standing the whole night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by how they still look for the metro maps on the walls instead of pulling them up on their phone, especially at Fontana.
